dag.description: Incremental PyTorch training with a SlipboxKnowledgeRouting step (DKS compile->index->route) feeding Bedrock BATCH forced-tool-use scoring, EDX anti-join, and EDX write-back. Batch variant of bedrock_slipbox_routing_pytorch_incremental: BedrockBatchProcessing_delta (async Bedrock batch inference) replaces the realtime BedrockProcessing_delta so a large DKS-routed delta is scored off the per-minute online quota. Two-source: baseline reads the scored-EDX label cache; delta reads live MDS anti-joined vs that cache.

metadata.name: bedrock_slipbox_routing_batch_pytorch_incremental
metadata.description: Incremental PyTorch training with a SlipboxKnowledgeRouting step (DKS compile->index->route) feeding Bedrock BATCH forced-tool-use scoring, EDX anti-join, and EDX write-back.

cost.estimated_hours: 4-24 (Bedrock batch delta scoring is async; routing + batch turnaround dominate)

agent_context.when_to_use: Same as bedrock_slipbox_routing_pytorch_incremental (incremental retraining of a DKS-routed LLM-labeled model, e.g. RnR) BUT the delta is LARGE (>~100K new orders) and the realtime BedrockProcessing path is bound by the account's per-minute Sonnet quota (ThrottlingException, multi-day wall). BedrockBatchProcessing_delta submits the routed records as an async Bedrock batch job (off the online RPM/TPM quota, ~50% cheaper for large volumes) then merges the scored labels back exactly like the realtime variant.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Small delta (<~100K orders) or when you need low-latency turnaround — use the realtime bedrock_slipbox_routing_pytorch_incremental (no async batch wall). First-time training (no scored-EDX baseline) — use the baseline dag.json first. If the labeling is a simple prompt with no DKS knowledge corpus / semantic routing, use bedrock_batch_pytorch_e2e (no SlipboxKnowledgeRouting step).
differentiators: Only incremental DAG that pairs SlipboxKnowledgeRouting (DKS compile->index->route) with Bedrock BATCH inference
Async batch scoring escapes the realtime per-minute quota that caps the online BedrockProcessing_delta delta size
BedrockBatchProcessing_delta consumes BOTH SlipboxKnowledgeRouting outputs (routed processed_data + the {ruleset,rules} prompt_templates)
EDX anti-join skips already-scored orders; EDX write-back grows the label cache
Delta feeds BOTH the EDX write-back and the training merge

prerequisites.check: Scored-EDX label cache exists from a prior run (or a prior backfill)
prerequisites.how: Verify the scored-label EDX ARN is accessible via Cradle
IAM role has bedrock:CreateModelInvocationJob for the target model
SlipboxKnowledgeRouting bundles the rule_*/pattern_*/behavior_* corpus in its source_dir (default), or mount it via the optional knowledge_corpus channel (DummyDataLoading S3)
Cradle date range covers a period NOT already in the scored EDX

common_pitfalls: BedrockBatchProcessing_delta MUST set bedrock_use_structured_output=true (forced tool-use) or the delta labels drift from the baseline labels
Batch jobs are async — the delta scoring step's wall-clock time is 4-24h; size the step max_runtime accordingly

Batch output is JSONL (differs from the realtime response) — ensure the batch result parser handles the batch response format; if a batch job fails mid-way partial results may exist in S3, handle idempotently
EdxUploading_scored must write the SAME 16-field reversal_predict.json schema the anti-join reads (positional headerless EDX — count+order must match)
knowledge_corpus is OPTIONAL (bundled default) — do NOT wire it unless overriding the corpus via DummyDataLoading
MDS date range must NOT overlap the scored EDX period — causes an empty anti-join result
Cradle transform_sql final projection must equal output_schema (no SELECT *; window/helper columns projected away)

has_prior_scored_edx?.yes: USE THIS DAG (scores only new orders)
has_prior_scored_edx?.no: Use the baseline dag.json first to seed the EDX cache

delta_volume_large_or_quota_bound?.yes: USE THIS DAG (async Bedrock batch escapes the realtime per-minute quota)
